label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to visualize this. You start at a point, take 6 steps forward. Then you turn left. Now, when you turn left, your direction changes 90 degrees to the left. So if you were initially facing north, after turning left you'd be facing west. After turning left, you take 4 steps. Then you take 4 more steps in the same direction (since the instructions just say "take 4 steps" again). So in total, after turning left, you take 4 + 4 = 8 steps in that new direction. So the path is: - Start at point A - Move forward 6 steps to point B - Turn left 90 degrees - Move forward 8 steps to point C Now, to determine if you end up back at the starting point, we need to see if the path forms a closed loop. In this case, the path is a right-angled turn from 6 steps to 8 steps. This doesn't form a closed loop unless there's another turn back.
